name: Apply a 10% loyalty discount on top of a multi-line quote
description: User asks for a 10% discount across the whole quote. The assistant should compute and add a discount line (negative amount), not silently rewrite individual lines.

persona.name: Lena Park
persona.context: Repeat customer, second job this year. Lena gives 10% off as a thank-you.

preconditions: Draft quote in current thread totals $3,200 across three lines

steps.action: Types: 'Give them 10% off as a loyalty discount.'
steps.expect: Assistant adds a 'Loyalty discount (10%)' line at -$320.00; new total $2,880.00

steps.action: Types: 'Make it a flat $300 off instead.'
steps.expect: Discount line updates to -$300.00; total $2,900.00 — original three lines untouched

evaluationCriteria.check: Negative line items are clearly styled as deductions (e.g. green or with a minus sign), not confusable with regular lines
Discount math is visible — user can sanity-check the percentage
Stored as amountCents = -32000 / -30000; subtotal/total computed downstream not pre-baked
Currency negatives are read as 'minus three hundred and twenty dollars', not '-320'
